For some reason, none of the screens that Gemini implemented resemble the actual pencil designs. 

Here are some guidelines from pencil to convert design to code:
Export Design to Code
Pencil automatically installs pencil MCP (Model Context Protocol) server to Claude Code, Codex and other coding agents allowing you to generate production-ready code from your designs directly in your terminal.

Active MCPs:

Claude Code CLI
Codex CLI
Gemini CLI
OpenCode CLI
Kiro CLI
Claude Desktop
How to export your design to code:

Pencil app has to be launched first, after that open Claude Code in your terminal by running claude
Check if you can see Pencil MCP tools by running command /mcp
Note: If you still don't see Pencil MCP tools, make sure to launch Pencil app first and then claude code afterwards. Pencil installs MCP tools automatically on launch.
Ask Claude to generate code from your designs (Keep Pencil desktop app running). Claude Code will be able to access selections, components, variables, etc.
Example prompt (Run via Claude Code CLI in Terminal):

Generate React/Tailwind/NextJS code from the selected frame
Update CSS based on the variables in the design
Create a React component based on the selected frame
Claude Code MCP setup showing Pencil MCP connected

Try converting this screen first:
Node ID: w7exk

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have the visual design and the structure. Let me analyze the design:

**PLAYGROUND - LIGHT** (w7exk) - 1728×1117px frame

The design shows:
1. **Background**: Linear gradient from `#fdf5e6` (top) to `#e8dcda` (bottom) — this is the Sonata light palette
2. **"HarmonyForge" title**: Large serif text (Fraunces, 57px), color `#2D1817`, positioned at top center
3. **Music Stand shape** (Frame 22, `NU4Ck`): 1439×648px, clipped with rounded top corners (20px radius), positioned at x:146, y:247


4. **Upload drop zone** centered on the stand with an upload icon, "Upload Score" label in IBM Plex Mono, supporting text showing the accepted file formats, and a dashed border to indicate the interactive area

.

5. **Stand base** at the bottom consisting of a rounded tan bar across the full width and a centered stem below it in a slightly darker tan — this creates the lectern shape of a music stand.

Now I'm planning the overall structure: a full viewport with the gradient background, the "HarmonyForge" title at the top in Fraunces font, and the music stand metaphor as the main focal point.

The stand desk itself is a trapezoid that's wider at the top (around 1439px) and narrows toward the bottom, with the upload drop zone centered inside it.

For the CSS approach, I'm considering using `clip-path: polygon()` to create the trapezoid shape, or possibly a perspective transform. The component structure would have the desk area with rounded top corners, the dashed upload zone in the center, and below that a horizontal bar connecting to a vertical stem.

Building the React component...
</details></think>

```text
    62→  --hf-detail:          #d2b48c;
    63→  --hf-accent:          #ffb300;
    64→  --hf-text-primary:    #1a1a1a;
    65→  --hf-text-secondary:  #6b5740;
    66→  --hf-detail-rgb:      210, 180, 140;
    67→
    68→  /* Staff lines use detail color per design system */
    69→  --hf-staff-line:      #d2b48c;
    70→  /* Canvas bg slightly off-white for score paper feel */
    71→  --hf-canvas-bg:       #faf0dc;
    72→  /* Toolbar/dock surface */
    73→  --hf-toolbar-bg:      rgba(253, 245, 230, 0.92);
    74→  --hf-toolbar-border:  rgba(210, 180, 140, 0.4);
    75→}
    76→
    77→/* ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    78→   Nocturne — Dark Mode
    79→   MASTER.md §1: Dark Walnut bg, Cognac surface
    80→   ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    81→.dark {
    82→  --hf-bg:              #3e2723;
    83→  --hf-surface:         #a55b37;
    84→  --hf-detail:          #2d1817;
    85→  --hf-accent:          #ffb300;
    86→  --hf-text-primary:    #f8f8f8;
    87→  --hf-text-secondary:  #b0a090;
    88→  --hf-detail-rgb:      45, 24, 23;
    89→
    90→  --hf-staff-line:      #5a3a30;
    91→  --hf-canvas-bg:       #2a1a16;
    92→  --hf-toolbar-bg:      rgba(62, 39, 35, 0.92);
    93→  --hf-toolbar-border:  rgba(45, 24, 23, 0.6);
    94→}
    95→
    96→/* ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    97→   Base resets & global rules
    98→   ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    99→*, *::before, *::after {
   100→  box-sizing: border-box;
   101→}
   102→
   103→html, body {
   104→  height: 100%;
   105→  overflow: hidden;
   106→  background-color: var(--hf-bg);
   107→  color: var(--hf-text-primary);
   108→  font-family: var(--font-body);
   109→  -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
   110→  -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale;
   111→}
   112→
   113→/* Focus rings — POUR, MASTER.md §7 */
   114→:focus-visible {
   115→  outline: 2px solid var(--hf-accent);
   116→  outline-offset: 2px;
   117→}
   118→
   119→/* Skip-to-content — POUR, MASTER.md §7 */
   120→.skip-link {
   121→  position: absolute;
   122→  top: -9999px;
   123→  left: 50%;
   124→  transform: translateX(-50%);
   125→  background: var(--hf-accent);
   126→  color: #1a1a1a;
   127→  padding: 8px 16px;
   128→  border-radius: var(--radius-standard);
   129→  font-family: var(--font-body);
   130→  font-size: 0.833rem;
   131→  font-weight: 500;
   132→  z-index: var(--z-critical);
   133→  text-decoration: none;
   134→}
   135→.skip-link:focus {
   136→  top: 8px;
   137→}
   138→
   139→/* Reduced motion guard — MASTER.md §5 */
   140→@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
   141→  *, *::before, *::after {
   142→    transition-duration: 0ms !important;
   143→    animation-duration: 0ms !important;
   144→  }
   145→}
   146→
   147→/* Score canvas — sharp edges, no radius per MASTER.md §3 */
   148→.score-canvas-container {
   149→  border-radius: var(--radius-sharp);
   150→  background-color: var(--hf-canvas-bg);
   151→}
   152→
   153→/* VexFlow SVG inherits text color correctly */
   154→.score-canvas-container svg text {
   155→  fill: var(--hf-text-primary);
   156→}
   157→.score-canvas-container svg .vf-stavenote path,
   158→.score-canvas-container svg .vf-stave path {
   159→  stroke: var(--hf-text-primary);
   160→  fill: var(--hf-text-primary);
   161→}
   162→
```
</details></tool-use>

# 🐝 Claude-Flow Swarm Coordination

Advanced multi-agent coordination system with timeout-free execution, distributed memory sharing, and intelligent load balancing.

## Basic Usage
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "your complex task" --strategy <type> [options]
```

## 🎯 Swarm Strategies
- **auto** - Automatic strategy selection based on task analysis
- **development** - Code implementation with review and testing
- **research** - Information gathering and synthesis
- **analysis** - Data processing and pattern identification
- **testing** - Comprehensive quality assurance
- **optimization** - Performance tuning and refactoring
- **maintenance** - System updates and bug fixes

## 🤖 Agent Types
- **coordinator** - Plans and delegates tasks to other agents
- **developer** - Writes code and implements solutions
- **researcher** - Gathers and analyzes information
- **analyzer** - Identifies patterns and generates insights
- **tester** - Creates and runs tests for quality assurance
- **reviewer** - Performs code and design reviews
- **documenter** - Creates documentation and guides
- **monitor** - Tracks performance and system health
- **specialist** - Domain-specific expert agents

## 🔄 Coordination Modes
- **centralized** - Single coordinator manages all agents (default)
- **distributed** - Multiple coordinators share management
- **hierarchical** - Tree structure with nested coordination
- **mesh** - Peer-to-peer agent collaboration
- **hybrid** - Mixed coordination strategies

## ⚙️ Common Options
- `--strategy <type>` - Execution strategy
- `--mode <type>` - Coordination mode
- `--max-agents <n>` - Maximum concurrent agents (default: 5)
- `--timeout <minutes>` - Timeout in minutes (default: 60)
- `--background` - Run in background for tasks > 30 minutes
- `--monitor` - Enable real-time monitoring
- `--ui` - Launch terminal UI interface
- `--parallel` - Enable parallel execution
- `--distributed` - Enable distributed coordination
- `--review` - Enable peer review process
- `--testing` - Include automated testing
- `--encryption` - Enable data encryption
- `--verbose` - Detailed logging output
- `--dry-run` - Show configuration without executing

## 🌟 Examples

### Development Swarm with Review
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Build e-commerce REST API" \
  --strategy development \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing
```

### Long-Running Research Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Analyze AI market trends 2024-2025" \
  --strategy research \
  --background \
  --distributed \
  --max-agents 8
```

### Performance Optimization Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Optimize database queries and API performance" \
  --strategy optimization \
  --testing \
  --parallel \
  --monitor
```

### Enterprise Development Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Implement secure payment processing system" \
  --strategy development \
  --mode distributed \
  --max-agents 10 \
  --parallel \
  --monitor \
  --review \
  --testing \
  --encryption \
  --verbose
```

### Testing and QA Swarm
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Comprehensive security audit and testing" \
  --strategy testing \
  --review \
  --verbose \
  --max-agents 6
```

## 📊 Monitoring and Control

### Real-time monitoring:
```bash
# Monitor swarm activity
./claude-flow monitor

# Monitor specific component
./claude-flow monitor --focus swarm
```

### Check swarm status:
```bash
# Overall system status
./claude-flow status

# Detailed swarm status
./claude-flow status --verbose
```

### View agent activity:
```bash
# List all agents
./claude-flow agent list

# Agent details
./claude-flow agent info <agent-id>
```

## 💾 Memory Integration

Swarms automatically use distributed memory for collaboration:

```bash
# Store swarm objectives
./claude-flow memory store "swarm_objective" "Build scalable API" --namespace swarm

# Query swarm progress
./claude-flow memory query "swarm_progress" --namespace swarm

# Export swarm memory
./claude-flow memory export swarm-results.json --namespace swarm
```

## 🎯 Key Features

### Timeout-Free Execution
- Background mode for long-running tasks
- State persistence across sessions
- Automatic checkpoint recovery

### Work Stealing & Load Balancing
- Dynamic task redistribution
- Automatic agent scaling
- Resource-aware scheduling

### Circuit Breakers & Fault Tolerance
- Automatic retry with exponential backoff
- Graceful degradation
- Health monitoring and recovery

### Real-Time Collaboration
- Cross-agent communication
- Shared memory access
- Event-driven coordination

### Enterprise Security
- Role-based access control
- Audit logging
- Data encryption
- Input validation

## 🔧 Advanced Configuration

### Dry run to preview:
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "Test task" --dry-run --strategy development
```

### Custom quality thresholds:
```bash
./claude-flow swarm "High quality API" \
  --strategy development \
  --quality-threshold 0.95
```

### Scheduling algorithms:
- FIFO (First In, First Out)
- Priority-based
- Deadline-driven
- Shortest Job First
- Critical Path
- Resource-aware
- Adaptive

For detailed documentation, see: https://github.com/ruvnet/claude-code-flow/docs/swarm-system.md

# Plan: Convert Pencil Design (w7exk — PLAYGROUND - LIGHT) to Code

## Context
The current `src/app/page.tsx` was implemented without reference to the Pencil design file.
It diverges from the source design in shape, typography, layout, gradient values, and the
stand base structure. This plan replaces the existing implementation with a pixel-faithful
translation of node `w7exk` ("PLAYGROUND - LIGHT") from `HarmonyForge.pen`.

---

## Design Source Analysis (node w7exk, 1728 × 1117px canvas)

| Element | Design Value | Current Code | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Frame bg | gradient `#fdf5e6` → `#e8dcda` (top→bottom) | `from-[#fdf5e6] to-[#e8dcda]` ✅ | Keep |
| Wordmark font | **Fraunces**, 57px, `#2D1817` | `font-brand` (Instrument Serif) ❌ | Fix + flag |
| Stand shape | Flat rect, 1439×648, rounded **top** corners (r:20), gradient `#fdf5e6`→`#d2b48c` | 3D `perspective + rotateX(25deg)`, wrong gradient (`--hf-surface` = cherry red) ❌ | Rewrite |
| Upload zone | 474×566, dashed border `#7a6b69`, 1px | 480×320, dashed, different color ❌ | Fix sizing |
| Upload icon | Custom SVG path, stroke `#1b1110`, w:7, round cap/join | `lucide-react UploadCloud` ❌ | Replace with SVG |
| "Upload Score" text | **IBM Plex Mono**, 50px, weight 500, `#1b1110` | `font-brand`, 2xl, wrong weight ❌ | Fix |
| "PDF/XML/MIDI" text | **IBM Plex Mono**, 30px, regular, `#7a6b69` | `font-mono uppercase tracking-widest` ❌ | Fix |
| Stand base | Horizontal bar 70px `#dec7a7` + vertical stem 82×288 `#d2b48c` | Generic "Piano Ledge" blurred div ❌ | Rewrite |

IBM Plex Mono is used for upload zone content — not currently imported. Must be added.
